#             work_order.insert()  # Save the work order
#     frappe.msgprint("Work orders created successfully!")


from frappe.model.naming import get_default_naming_series

def autoworkorder(doc):
    try:
        # Check if Work Order already exists
        if frappe.db.exists("Work Order", {"sales_order": doc.name}):
            frappe.msgprint("Work Order already exists for this Sales Order.")
            return
        
        # Create Work Order
        work_order = frappe.new_doc("Work Order")
        work_order.naming_series = get_default_naming_series("Work Order")
        work_order.sales_order = doc.name
        work_order.item_code = doc.items[0].item_code
        work_order.qty = doc.items[0].qty
        work_order.insert(ignore_permissions=True)
        work_order.submit()
        
        frappe.msgprint("Work Order created successfully!")
    
    except Exception as e:
        frappe.log_error(e)
        frappe.msgprint("Error creating Work Order: " + str(e))

name: work order status
CS_Quotation
script:
# Example Workflow Customization (frappe.custom_script)
def create_quotation_from_so(doc, method):
    if doc.docstatus == 1:  # Submitted SO
        quotation = frappe.new_doc("Quotation")
        quotation.customer = doc.customer
        for item in doc.items:
            quotation.append("items", {
                "item_code": item.item_code,
                "qty": item.qty,
                "rate": item.rate
            })
        quotation.insert(ignore_permissions=True)
        frappe.msgprint(f"Quotation {quotation.name} created")

# Hook this to Sales Order's "on_submit" event

name: timestamp job card
script:
# Mapping of workflow states to custom datetime fields
state_field_mapping = {
    'Fabrication In Process': 'custom_fabrication_start',
    'Fabrication Complete': 'custom_fabrication_end',
    'Copper Platting in Progress': 'custom_platting_start',
    'Plating Completed': 'custom_platting_end',
    'Copper Cutting In Process': 'custom_cutting_start',
    'Cutting Completed': 'custom_cutting_end',
    'Polishing in Process': 'custom_polishing_start',
    'Polishing Completed': 'custom_polishing_end',
    'Engraving in Process': 'custom_engraving_start',
    'Engraving Complete': 'custom_engraving_end',
    'Chrome In Progress': 'custom_chrome_start',
    'Chrome Completed': 'custom_chrome_end',
    'Proofing In Progress': 'custom_proofing_start',
    'Proofing Completed': 'custom_proofing_end',
#    'Completed': 'custom_end_of_process'
}

current_state = doc.workflow_state

if current_state in state_field_mapping:
    fieldname = state_field_mapping[current_state]
    doc.set(fieldname, frappe.utils.now_datetime())

name: AutoStartJobCard
script:
# Get the job card document
job_card = frappe.get_doc('Job Card', doc.name)

# Update the status and started time
job_card.status = 'Work In Progress'
job_card.started_time = frappe.utils.now()

# Save the document (this will trigger all validations and events)
job_card.save(ignore_permissions=True)

name: FatchBatchDelivery
script:
for item in doc.items:
    if item.against_sales_order:
        # Find all batches linked to this new sales order for this item
        batches = frappe.db.get_all(
            "Batch",
            filters={
                "custom_new_sales_order": item.against_sales_order,
                "item": item.item_code
            },
            fields=["name"]
        )

        if not batches:
            frappe.msgprint(
                f"No batch found for Sales Order {item.against_sales_order} and item {item.item_code}. "
                "Please select batch manually.",
                alert=True
            )
            continue

        selected_batch = None
        selected_balance = 0

        for b in batches:
            # Use ERPNext's reliable stock balance function
            actual_balance = frappe.call(
                "erpnext.stock.utils.get_stock_balance",
                item_code=item.item_code,
                warehouse=item.warehouse,
                posting_date=doc.posting_date,
                posting_time=doc.posting_time,
                batch_no=b.name
            )

            if actual_balance >= item.qty:
                selected_batch = b.name
                selected_balance = actual_balance
                break

        if not selected_batch:
            # No batch with enough stock – pick the first and show warning with actual balance
            selected_batch = batches[0].name
            selected_balance = frappe.call(
                "erpnext.stock.utils.get_stock_balance",
                item_code=item.item_code,
                warehouse=item.warehouse,
                posting_date=doc.posting_date,
                posting_time=doc.posting_time,
                batch_no=selected_batch
            )
            frappe.msgprint(
                f"⚠️ No batch with sufficient stock for {item.item_code}. "
                f"Using batch {selected_batch} (available {selected_balance}). "
                "Please check stock or adjust manually.",
                alert=True, indicator="red"
            )

        item.batch_no = selected_batch
        frappe.msgprint(f"✅ Assigned batch {selected_batch} to item {item.item_code}")

name: GenerateBatchStockIn
get_pdf_hex
script:
doctype = frappe.form_dict.get('doctype')
name = frappe.form_dict.get('name')
print_format = frappe.form_dict.get('print_format')

if not doctype or not name:
    frappe.throw("doctype and name are required")

pdf = frappe.get_print(doctype, name, print_format=print_format or "Standard", as_pdf=True)
frappe.response['message'] = pdf.hex()   # return PDF bytes as hex string

name: send_email_with_attachments
diagnose_sales_order_permission
script:
def diagnose_sales_order_permission(sales_order, user_email):
    """Diagnose why a user cannot access a specific Sales Order"""
    response = {
        "user": user_email,
        "sales_order": sales_order,
        "roles": [],
        "user_permissions": [],
        "link_fields": [],
        "restricted_links": [],
        "permission_rules": [],
        "root_cause": None
    }
    
    # Get user's roles
    response["roles"] = frappe.get_roles(user_email)
    
    # Get User Permissions (document-level restrictions)
    response["user_permissions"] = frappe.get_all("User Permission", 
        filters={"user": user_email}, 
        fields=["allow", "for_value", "apply_to_all_doctypes", "applicable_for"])
    
    # As Administrator, get the Sales Order details
    frappe.set_user("Administrator")
    doc = frappe.get_doc("Sales Order", sales_order)
    
    # Check Role Permissions for Sales Order
    response["permission_rules"] = frappe.get_all("Custom DocPerm",
        filters={"parent": "Sales Order", "role": ["in", response["roles"]]},
        fields=["role", "read", "write", "if_owner", "apply_user_permissions"])
    
    # Identify all Link fields and check user's access to linked documents
    frappe.set_user(user_email)
    for field in doc.meta.fields:
        if field.fieldtype == "Link" and doc.get(field.fieldname):
            link_value = doc.get(field.fieldname)
            link_doctype = field.options
            
            response["link_fields"].append({
                "field": field.fieldname,
                "value": link_value,
                "doctype": link_doctype
            })
            
            # Try to read the linked document
            try:
                frappe.get_doc(link_doctype, link_value)
                # Accessible
            except frappe.PermissionError:
                response["restricted_links"].append({
                    "field": field.fieldname,
                    "value": link_value,
                    "doctype": link_doctype,
                    "error": "User cannot read this linked document"
                })
    
    # Determine root cause
    if response["restricted_links"]:
        response["root_cause"] = f"User lacks read permission on linked document(s): {', '.join([f['doctype'] for f in response['restricted_links']])}"
    elif response["user_permissions"]:
        response["root_cause"] = "User Permissions may be restricting access to this specific record"
    elif not any(rule.get("read") for rule in response["permission_rules"]):
        response["root_cause"] = "User's roles lack read permission on Sales Order"
    else:
        response["root_cause"] = "Unknown cause - check if 'Apply User Permissions' is enabled"
    
    return response

name: DeliveryNote_Update_SalesOrder_closed
script:
# This script runs after a Delivery Note is submitted
# It updates the linked Sales Order's custom_closed field with the latest Delivery Note posting_date

# Loop through all items in the Delivery Note
for item in doc.items:
    if item.against_sales_order:
        sales_order_name = item.against_sales_order
        
        # Get the Sales Order document
        sales_order = frappe.get_doc("Sales Order", sales_order_name)
        
        # Only update if custom_closed is not already set
        if not sales_order.custom_closed:
            # Find all submitted Delivery Notes linked to this Sales Order
            delivery_dates = frappe.db.sql("""
                SELECT MAX(dn.posting_date)
                FROM `tabDelivery Note Item` dni
                JOIN `tabDelivery Note` dn ON dn.name = dni.parent
                WHERE dni.against_sales_order = %s AND dn.docstatus = 1
            """, sales_order_name)
            
            if delivery_dates and delivery_dates[0][0]:
                closed_date = delivery_dates[0][0]
                # Update the Sales Order's custom_closed field without changing modified timestamp
                frappe.db.set_value(
                    "Sales Order",
                    sales_order_name,
                    "custom_closed",
                    closed_date,
                    update_modified=False
                )
                frappe.log_error(
                    title="Custom Closed Updated",
                    message=f"Sales Order {sales_order_name} set to {closed_date} from Delivery Note {doc.name}"
                )
